Most-Streamed Artists on Spotify

Taylor Swift ranks as the most-streamed act in Spotify history, with 125,317,639,553 total streams worldwide. Streaming dominates the music industry, accounting for roughly 69% of global recorded music sales, with Spotify holding the largest share.

Earning a spot among the top Spotify artists ranking isn’t just impressive; it marks global superstardom. This constantly updated ranking highlights the most listened-to artists on Spotify since its launch, based on lead artist streams. Check in daily for the latest stats and shifting positions.

Most-Streamed Artists vs Monthly Listeners

When it comes to measuring an artist’s popularity on Spotify, there’s often confusion between different metrics. For example, publications like Guinness World Records frequently highlight the artist with the most monthly listeners as the “most streamed” or “most popular” on the platform.

In reality, the most-streamed artists on Spotify are those who have amassed the highest total playcounts, reflecting their cumulative success over time. This distinction is significant because Spotify does not publicly share total stream counts for artists, focusing instead on monthly listeners, a metric that comes with technical limitations.

Spotify also uses a popularity index that ranks artists based on recent streams, but this data isn’t openly available. As a result, the true cumulative impact of artists on the platform often goes unnoticed.

Since 2020, this page has provided a clear and updated view of the artists who have generated the most streams on Spotify. With a daily refreshed top 1,000 list, it offers insight into long-term streaming trends rather than short-term fluctuations.

A Brief History of Spotify’s Artists’ Milestones

Back in late 2015, EDM artist Kygo made waves by becoming the first artist to reach 1 billion streams in a year, a record-breaking achievement at the time. Fast forward to today, and streaming milestones have reached unprecedented heights, with several artists now closing in on the remarkable 100 billion streams mark.

For years, Drake led the pack, becoming the first artist to achieve 30 billion streams in April 2020, followed by 40 billion in August 2021 and 50 billion in September 2022. By December 2023, his total had surpassed 70 billion.

However, in February 2024, Taylor Swift claimed the title of Spotify’s most-streamed artist of all time. Her meteoric rise has been nothing short of extraordinary: from 11.9 billion streams in 2020 to over 90 billion by late 2024, and over 100 billion by March 16, 2025. Notably, she was still trailing Ariana Grande among female artists as recently as a few years ago.

Today’s top three is rounded out by Bad Bunny, the leader among Latin artists, whose dominance reflects the global reach of reggaeton and Latin music. Taylor Swift and Bad Bunny have also consistently alternated as the platform’s most popular daily artists for several years. The latter led the list of 2025’s most-streamed artists.

More Top Artists

Among groups, BTS leads with their exceptional success in K-pop. In the realm of legacy acts, rock legends Queen remain the top performers, demonstrating the enduring appeal of classic hits.

When it comes to averages, Harry Styles stands out. Despite releasing only 36 distinct songs across his solo career, each one has exceeded 100 million streams, securing him a place among the top 40 most-streamed artists overall.

Most-Streamed Artists on Spotify by All Credits

Featured streams can be misleading, as some lesser-known acts see their playcounts skyrocket just by being part of a major hit. For established stars, however, whose influence strongly contributes to the success of nearly every track they feature on, these streams remain very relevant. The table below combines lead and featured streams, with Drake topping the list.

Most-Streamed Artists across all On-Demand Audio Platforms

While Spotify dominates the streaming conversation, it’s essential to note that other major platforms, such as Apple Music and Amazon Music, are also relevant. Yet, they don’t share playcount data publicly.

To bridge this gap, we’ve set up advanced algorithms to estimate comprehensive on-demand audio streams across platforms. These calculations offer a broader view of an artist’s global streaming footprint.

This ranking is a reflection of years of analysis, consistently validated against certified results. It offers fans and industry professionals alike a reliable snapshot of streaming achievements.

Explore the full ranking below to see where your favorite artist stands and gain a deeper understanding of the streaming world.

Most-Streamed Artists on Audio DSPs – How it Works

We estimate audio-on-demand (AOD) streams across all major Digital Streaming Platforms (DSPs) to determine the most-streamed artists globally.

This ranking reflects the top artists based on estimated career streams from AOD platforms such as Spotify, Apple Music, Amazon Music, Deezer, YouTube Music, and notable regional services like Anghami, Gaana, Joox, Melon, JioSaavn, Boomplay, among others. Radio-style platforms (e.g., iHeartRadio, Pandora) and video platforms are excluded from our data.

Because most DSPs do not publicly share playcounts, and labels themselves only receive consolidated figures several months later, we rely on estimates to deliver a comprehensive view of an artist’s streaming performance.

How do we Estimate Streams?

Our methodology involves:

  • Analyzing Spotify’s market share in over 100 countries
  • Assessing each artist’s streaming distribution by market
  • Factoring in how music genres perform differently across platforms

These elements enable us to calculate the percentage of an artist’s global streams that originate from Spotify. We then extrapolate that percentage to estimate their total AOD streams across all platforms, based on known Spotify figures.

Only leading tracks are included in these calculations. Featured appearances are excluded.

Q&A

… about Spotify’s Most-Streamed Artists

What is this ranking about? What are lead and featured streams?

This ranking showcases the top Spotify artists based on streams from tracks listed under their personal page’s Albums, Singles, and Compilations sections. These are combined into what we define as “Lead Streams.”
Milestones such as the number of tracks available and total streams are calculated using lead songs only. Streams from tracks listed in the “Appears On” section of an artist’s Spotify page are categorized as “Featured Streams” and do not impact the overall ranking or milestone statistics.

Who is the most-streamed artist of all time?

The most-streamed artist of all time is American icon Taylor Swift, followed by Latin star Bad Bunny and Canadian rapper Drake.

Who is the most-streamed solo female artist of all time?

Taylor Swift also holds the crown as the most-streamed solo female artist of all time. She is followed by Ariana Grande and Billie Eilish, with Rihanna and Dua Lipa rounding out the top five.

Who is the most-streamed group of all time?

The most-streamed group of all time is South Korean global sensation BTS, ahead of Coldplay and Imagine Dragons.

Who is the most-streamed rapper of all time?

The most-streamed rapper of all time is Canadian hip-hop star Drake. The top five most-listened-to rappers also include Eminem, Kanye West, Travis Scott, and Juice WRLD, in that order.

Who is the most-streamed rock artist of all time

Rock legends Queen take the top spot as the most-streamed rock artist of all time. They are followed by Linkin Park, Arctic Monkeys, The Beatles, and Red Hot Chili Peppers.

Who is the most-streamed Asian artist of all time

The most-streamed Asian artist of all time is the South Korean group BTS. Indian superstars Arijit Singh and Pritam complete the top three.

Who is the most-streamed Latin artist of all time

Latin music’s king, Bad Bunny, holds the top spot among Latin artists, followed by J Balvin and Ozuna.

What is the most-streamed album of all time?

The most-streamed album of all time is Bad Bunny‘s Un Verano Sin Ti. Explore the full top 5,000 right now.

What is the most-streamed song of all time?

The most-streamed song (across all versions) and the most-streamed track (with no side version) is Blinding Lights by The Weeknd, taking the lead over Shape of You by Ed Sheeran.

There is an issue with the data. How does it get fixed?

Spotify’s database can occasionally be tricky to manage, so while we’ve developed robust controls to ensure accuracy, occasional glitches may still occur.
When issues arise, we perform manual fixes to restore data consistency. If a problem persists, don’t hesitate to reach out and let us know so we can investigate further.

… about Most-Streamed Artists on Audio DSPs

Are these numbers official?

No. Outside of Spotify, most DSPs don’t disclose track playcounts. Even music labels face a delay in accessing complete data.
Our algorithm uses Spotify as a foundation (see How Do We Estimate Streams?) and applies a set of data-driven extrapolations to estimate each artist’s total audio streams across all platforms. These estimates form the basis of the rankings displayed.

What if some numbers seem off?

People often focus on platforms they know, like Apple Music, especially for urban music popular in English-speaking countries. While Apple Music may outperform Spotify for some artists in the US, it represents only a small fraction of global streaming.

For instance, Drake and Beyoncé may perform better on US Apple Music than US Spotify, but in Brazil, Spotify holds a dominant market share. Therefore, Beyoncé‘s non-Spotify share is smaller than Drake‘s due to regional dynamics.

This explains why:
– Latin and Brazilian artists often rank lower, as Spotify dominates in their regions.
– Asian artists and global stars with strong performance in Asia may rank higher, thanks to powerful local platforms like Melon, Joox, or Genie.

Why does this data differ from CSPC articles?

Both use the same core methodology, but CSPC entries incorporate additional manual data collection from platforms like Genie, AWA, and Anghami. As a result, CSPC figures feature a higher proportion of verified data, though differences from the estimates remain relatively minor.

Why isn’t a particular artist listed?

The top of the ranking is comprehensive, but some lower-ranked artists may be missing. You can use our streaming data tool to search for any artist. If they have enough streams, they will automatically appear in the ranking, which is updated in real-time as new data arrives.
